name: Amapa (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the people who died for the territory

description: Three horizontal bands of blue, white and yellow with a green diagonal stripe rising from the lower hoist to the upper fly along the white band, charged toward the hoist with a black silhouette of the Fortress of Sao Jose de Macapa.
history: Adopted on 23 April 1984 during the territorial period; the design is by Anibal de Vasconcelos Barreto and was retained when Amapa became a state in 1988.

features.symbolism: Three horizontal stripes, with a black silhouette of the Fortress of Sao Jose de Macapa overlapping the stripes near the hoist; a green diagonal band sweeps from the upper hoist to the lower fly, evoking the lands of Amapa

features.role: Fortress of Sao Jose de Macapa
features.symbolism: the colonial-era Fortress of Sao Jose de Macapa, a defining historical landmark of the state
